past_projects.name: Frank Lloyd Wright Ennis House - concrete textile block testing
past_projects.historic_context: Built c.1924 by Frank Lloyd Wright; listed on the National Register of Historic Places and designated a California Historical Landmark. The house suffered damage in the 1994 Northridge earthquake and, in 2005, torrential rains caused mudslides that collapsed sections of its terraced retaining wall, prompting emergency red-tagging and its listing on the National Trust's '11 Most Endangered Historic Places.' A multi-year stabilization/restoration campaign (2006-2011) led by the Ennis House Foundation followed, and AMT's 2007 concrete-block testing was performed within that disaster-recovery restoration effort.
